enonce.texte: Read the following direct speech sentences and rewrite them in reported speech.

questions.texte: "I am going to the market," said John.
questions.reponse: John said that he was going to the market.

questions.texte: "We have finished our homework," the students said.
questions.reponse: The students said that they had finished their homework.

questions.texte: "I will help you tomorrow," she promised.
questions.reponse: She promised that she would help me the next day.

enonce.texte: Match the vocabulary words with their definitions.

questions.texte: Refund
Warranty
Complaint
Customer service
Option

enonce.texte:
Read the following conversation and rewrite the customer's statements in reported speech.

Conversation: Customer: "I bought this phone yesterday. It doesn't work properly. I want a refund."
Customer service: "I am sorry to hear that. Let me check your warranty."

questions.texte: What did the customer say about the phone?
questions.reponse: The customer said that he/she had bought the phone the previous day and that it didn't work properly.

questions.texte: What did the customer say they wanted?
questions.reponse: The customer said that they wanted a refund.

questions.texte: What did the customer service representative say?
questions.reponse: The representative said that they were sorry to hear that and would check the warranty.

enonce.texte: Write a formal email of complaint about a faulty online purchase. Include the following elements: subject, greeting, details of the problem, request for action, and closing.

questions.texte: Write the email.
questions.reponse:
Subject: Complaint about faulty product
Dear Customer Service,
I am writing to complain about a laptop I bought online last week. The device does not turn on and I am very disappointed. I would like to request a refund or replacement. Please let me know how to proceed.
Yours sincerely,
[Your Name]

enonce.texte:
Read the following user manual instructions and answer the questions.

Instructions: 1. Turn on the device by pressing the power button. 2. Wait for the screen to light up. 3. Select the language you prefer. 4. Follow the setup wizard to complete the installation.

questions.texte: What is the first step?
questions.reponse: Turn on the device by pressing the power button.

questions.texte: What should you do after the screen lights up?
What is the purpose of the setup wizard?
questions.reponse: To complete the installation of the device.

intitule: Completing Sentences with Customer Service Vocabulary

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct words: refund, warranty, complaint, customer service, option.

questions.texte: If you are not satisfied, you can ask for a ______.
The product has a one-year ______.
She filed a ______ about the poor service.
The ______ team helped me solve the problem.
We have the ______ to choose between delivery or pick-up.

enonce.texte:
Read the following automated telephone message and answer the questions.

Message: "Welcome to our customer service. For account balance, press 1. For payment issues, press 2. For technical support, press 3. To speak to a representative, press 0. Please listen carefully to all options before making a selection."

questions.texte: What should you press for payment issues?
How can you speak to a representative?
What advice does the message give?
questions.reponse: Listen carefully to all options before making a selection.

intitule: Applying Reported Speech to Relay Information

enonce.texte:
Read the following announcement and relay the information using reported speech.

Announcement: "The Mayor announced, 'We will be sorting out our garbage collection system next month. Citizens will receive new schedules and bins.'"

questions.texte: Relay the Mayor's announcement in reported speech.
questions.reponse: The Mayor announced that they would be sorting out their garbage collection system the following month. Citizens would receive new schedules and bins.

intitule: Creating a Dialogue for Customer Service

enonce.texte: Create a short dialogue between a customer and a customer service representative. Include the following: the customer describes a problem, the representative offers a solution, and the customer agrees or asks for clarification.

questions.texte: Write the dialogue.
questions.reponse:
Customer: Hello, I have a problem with my order. It arrived damaged.
Representative: I am sorry to hear that. Would you like a refund or a replacement?
Customer: I would prefer a replacement. How long will it take?
Representative: It will take about 3-5 business days. Is that acceptable?
Customer: Yes, thank you for your help.

enonce.texte: Read each sentence and choose the correct meaning of the underlined word.

questions.texte: The <u>warranty</u> covers any defects for one year.
I would like to request a <u>refund</u>.
She filed a <u>complaint</u> about the service.
